brand.name: ThinkBrowse
brand.essence: Professional AI tooling — warm, precise, status-driven
brand.description: A developer tool for monitoring and controlling AI browser automation sessions. The design is calm, readable, and transparent about agent state — warm cream base softens an otherwise technical UI.

designPrinciples.name: Status Clarity
designPrinciples.description: Every state (running, blocked, completed, failed) must be immediately legible. Color, iconography, and text all reinforce status simultaneously.

designPrinciples.name: Transparent Intelligence
designPrinciples.description: Show the agent's thinking in a structured execution log. Use numbered entries, action-type badges, and THINK entries in italic to make the agent's reasoning readable.

designPrinciples.name: Warm Precision
designPrinciples.description: Cream background (#FDFBF9) instead of pure white. Sharp Inter typography for data, Instrument Serif only for page-level titles. No glassmorphism, no floating animations.

designPrinciples.name: Static Layouts
designPrinciples.description: Panels are fixed-position with internal scroll. No floating, no transform animations on structural elements. Only pulse animations for live status indicators.

headings.usage: Page/session titles only (h1 in the header). Not for section labels or panel headers.

monospace.usage: Session IDs, timestamps, URLs, action badge labels, code snippets

sectionLabels.usage: TASK PLAN, EXECUTION LOG, AGENT OBSERVABILITY panel headings

layout.pageStructure: Full viewport height (h-screen), header fixed at top (h-16), main area fills remainder (flex-1 overflow-hidden)
layout.rightColumn: flex flex-col, bg-white — fixed header, scrollable body (flex-1 overflow-y-auto p-6)
layout.spacing: p-6 (24px) for panel padding, mb-6 (24px) between major sections, mb-8 (32px) between right panel sections

allowed: animate-pulse — status dots and blocked-state indicators only
forbidden: animate-float — no floating on any structural element
backdrop-blur / glassmorphism — not used in session detail
scale transforms on panels or browser frame
